Appearances (by Zoom):
Adron Beene Jr, Esq. 408-392-9233 for Plaintiff.
Tracy Zinsou. Esq. 415-599-0210 Braunhagey & Borden LLP for Defendant.
Court reporter: Darla Kmety, CSR #12956, 858-735-4744, darlakmety@gmal.com.

RULING - A R G U E D. Ms. Zinsous argues. The Court shares its views. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RICHARD INTRATER, AN INDIVIDUAL, LTS CAPITAL PARTNERS--FALLBROOK VI, LLC, LTS CAPITAL PARTNERS--FALLBROOK VII, LLC, RICHARD INTRATER, LONG-TERM SOLUTIONS, LLC's Motion To Require Out-Of-State Plaintiffs To File Undertaking.

Defendants' "motion to require out-of-state plaintiff to file undertaking" is denied without prejudice. To "obtain an order requiring such bond, defendant must show a 'reasonable probability' that it will prevail at trial, and a detailed estimate of the costs it is likely to incur." (Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 11:280.2.) The summary adjudication hearing set for February 22, 2023 may well shed light on both required showings. (See Zinsou Dec. 4:8.) At present, the cost estimate is far from detailed. (Id. at 5:8-9.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Darla Kmety, CSR #12956, 858-735-4744, darlakmety@gmal.com. Reported. (302/rbu)
